svg.text: BIBLICAL HEBREW
GRAMMAR — SEMITIC ROOT SYSTEM
THREE-CONSONANT ROOT (SHORESH)
Root K-T-B generates: write, writing, writer, written, office...
katav "he wrote" • kotev "writing/writer" • ktav "script"
mikhtav "letter" • ktuvim "writings" (same root, different pattern)
Patterns (binyanim) shape meaning: simple, causative, intensive, passive
BIBLICAL HEBREW VERB SYSTEM
No true tense; instead: aspect (perfect = completed, imperfect = ongoing)
Waw-consecutive: vav prefix flips aspect (key to narrative prose)
wa-yiqtol = "and he [did]" • turns imperfect into past narrative
Seven binyanim: Qal, Nifal, Piel, Pual, Hitpael, Hifil, Hofal
BIBLICAL HEBREW WORD ORDER
Typical: VSO (Verb-Subject-Object) in narrative
Bereshit bara Elohim: "In-beginning created God..." (VSO)
Nominal sentences (no verb): topic-comment structure
ARABIC GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ARABIC THREE-CONSONANT ROOT
Root K-T-B in Arabic: kataba "he wrote"; kitab "book"; maktab "office"
IDENTICAL root to Hebrew K-T-B — same meaning, same consonants!
Arabic: 10 verb forms (awzan) vs Hebrew 7 binyanim (same concept)
ARABIC VERB AND CASE SYSTEM
Classical Arabic has 3 cases: nominative (-u), accusative (-a), genitive (-i)
Biblical Hebrew: no case endings (lost in early period)
Arabic dual: -ayn suffix (Hebrew has dual too, chiefly for paired things)
Arabic broken plural: kitab → kutub (internal vowel change, no suffix)
ARABIC WORD ORDER: VSO
Classical Arabic also VSO: kataba al-rajulu l-kitaba
"Wrote the-man the-book" = "The man wrote the book" (VSO)
Same verb-initial word order as Biblical Hebrew
PARALLEL SEMITIC GRAMMAR
Both: 3-consonant roots • both: internal vowel changes mark meaning
Both: VSO order • both: verb agrees with gender + number of subject
Many roots identical: K-T-B, Sh-L-M, M-L-K, D-B-R (same meanings)
PLATE 09c ALT · BIBLICAL HEBREW / ARABIC · GRAMMAR
